title: Revised Metrics Analytics Tab with Two-State Dashboard/Report Management
story: As a data analyst using the Metrics Analytics tab, I want a simplified two-state interface for managing related dashboards and reports, so that I can clearly see existing relationships and add new ones without getting lost.
description:
User observation revealed confusion with the current Analytics tab flow. This redesign simplifies the interface into two clear states.

## State 1 — Related Dashboards/Reports List (default when > 0 exist)
- Shows the list of currently related dashboards or reports
- Each item shows a Salesforce blue icon and blue link to open in Salesforce
- An "Add Dashboard" / "Add Report" button to switch to State 2

## State 2 — Relate Dashboard/Report (adding new relationships)
- Default state when 0 related items exist (no cancel button in this case)
- Shows the Salesforce selection interface to relate dashboards/reports
- A "Cancel" button appears only when the user toggled from State 1 — it navigates back to State 1
- A "Complete" button appears once at least one item is added

## Navigation Behavior
- Exiting the slide-out, toggling between dashboards/reports, or leaving the sub-tab resets back to State 1
- When deleting the last related item, automatically switches to State 2
- Updated to use `nav-pills` styling for the sub-navigation tabs
acceptance_criteria: When > 0 related dashboards exist, State 1 (list view) is shown by default
Salesforce items display with blue icon and blue link
Add Dashboard/Report button switches to State 2
When 0 related dashboards exist, State 2 (relate view) is shown by default without Cancel button
Cancel button appears only when toggled from State 1
Complete button appears once at least one item is added
Deleting the last related item switches to State 2 automatically
Exiting the slide-out or switching tabs resets to State 1
ux_changes: Analytics tab redesigned into two clear states: list view (showing related items with Salesforce links) and relate view (adding new relationships). Cancel/Complete button visibility is context-dependent. Nav-pills styling applied to sub-tabs.
middleware_changes: State management logic for tracking whether the user navigated from State 1 to State 2 (affects Cancel button visibility). Reset logic on slide-out exit and tab switching.
implementation_notes: Implemented Cancel button visibility based on whether user toggled from the list view. Complete button only visible when at least one item is added. Auto-reset to default state on slide-out exit or tab switch. Product owner requested nav-pills styling. Developer implemented checks for both reports and dashboards.
comment_summary: Developer implemented the two-state UI. Discussion about Cancel button semantics — developer noted Cancel could be confused with undoing additions. Complete button added conditionally. Product owner requested reset behavior on navigation and nav-pills styling update. Multiple rounds of review and refinement.
